ddls:
CREATE TABLE "robot_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data record */,
  "wall_time" TEXT /* Wall time (Unix timestamp) when the data was recorded */,
  "egm_time" TEXT /* Engine Management (EGM) time (Unix timestamp) when the data was recorded */,
  "egm_seqno" INTEGER /* Sequence number of the data record */,
  "input_joint" REAL /* Input joint position */,
  "received_joint" REAL /* Received joint position */,
  "robot_id" INTEGER /* ID of the robotic system */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manufacturing site */,
  "cycle_time" REAL /* Cycle time of the robotic system */,
  "production_rate" REAL /* Production rate of the robotic system */,
  "scrap_rate" REAL /* Scrap rate of the robotic system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_robot_data_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_robot_data_robot_id FOREIGN KEY ("robot_id") REFERENCES robots ("robot_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "robots" (
  "robot_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each robotic system */,
  "robot_name" TEXT /* Name of the robotic system */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the robotic system */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the robotic system */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the robotic system was installed */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manufacturing site where the robotic system is located */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the robotic system */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Firmware version of the robotic system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("robot_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_robots_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manufacturing site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the manufacturing site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the manufacturing site */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the site */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the site */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor (e.g., temperature, pressure) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the sensor on the robotic system */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "robot_id" INTEGER /* ID of the robotic system where the sensor is installed */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_robot_id FOREIGN KEY ("robot_id") REFERENCES robots ("robot_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "sensor_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor data record */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor */,
  "wall_time" TEXT /* Wall time (Unix timestamp) when the data was recorded */,
  "value" REAL /* Value recorded by the sensor */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the recorded value */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor (e.g., normal, warning, error) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance log */,
  "robot_id" INTEGER /* ID of the robotic system */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance was performed */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the maintenance activity (in minutes) */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of the maintenance activity (e.g., routine, corrective) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_robot_id FOREIGN KEY ("robot_id") REFERENCES robots ("robot_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES technicians ("technician_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "technicians" (
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each technician */,
  "technician_name" TEXT /* Name of the technician */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the technician */,
  "expertise" TEXT /* Area of expertise (e.g., mechanical, electrical) */,
  "certifications" TEXT /* Certifications held by the technician */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("technician_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., operator, engineer, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Password hash for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the platform was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource being accessed (e.g., robot, sensor, maintenance log) */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of the resource being accessed (e.g., robot, sensor, maintenance log) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarms" (
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm */,
  "robot_id" INTEGER /* ID of the robotic system */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor (if applicable) */,
  "alarm_time" TEXT /* Time when the alarm was generated (Unix timestamp) */,
  "alarm_type" TEXT /* Type of the alarm (e.g., temperature, pressure) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alarm */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alarm (e.g., active, cleared) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alarm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_robot_id FOREIGN KEY ("robot_id") REFERENCES robots ("robot_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_learning_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine learning model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine learning model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the machine learning model (e.g., regression, classification) */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date the model was trained */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the machine learning model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine learning model */,
  "robot_id" INTEGER /* ID of the robotic system */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date the prediction was made */,
  "predicted_value" REAL /* Predicted value */,
  "confidence" REAL /* Confidence in the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES machine_learning_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_robot_id FOREIGN KEY ("robot_id") REFERENCES robots ("robot_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of the notification (e.g., alarm, maintenance) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table being audited */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the record being audited */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date the change was made */,
  "field_name" TEXT /* Name of the field being changed */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the field */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the field */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id")
)
